feed.title: Current Watches, Warnings and Advisories for New Zealand issued by MetService
feed.rights: Copyright Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited, 2026. Licensed under Creative Commons BY 4.0.

entry.title: Heavy Rain Warning - Orange
entry.summary: Expect 75 to 100 mm of rain. The heaviest falls are likely Sunday evening and Monday morning when intensities of 10 mm/h are possible. Note, snow level lowering and snow is possible above 200 metres and there is a separate snow warning. Impact: Streams and rivers may rise rapidly. Surface flooding, slips, and difficult driving conditions possible. Chance of upgrading to a Red Warning: Low.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Heavy Snow Warning - Orange
entry.summary: Snow is expected to develop Sunday morning and the level lower during the day, possibly as low as 200 metres. Above 400 metres, expect over 15 cm of snow to accumulate, with 10 cm possible in 3 to 4 hours. Snow accumulations below 600 metres could reach 25 to 30 cm but may exceed 1 metre above 800 metres altitude, especially south of Queenstown to Middlemarch. Impact: Travel disruption and damage to trees and powerlines possible. Cold conditions may cause stress for livestock. Chance of upgrading to a Red Warning: Low.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Heavy Rain Watch
entry.summary: Periods of heavy rain and accumulations may approach warning criteria. Note, snow level lowering and there is a separate warning for heavy snowfall for northern Southland. Chance of upgrading to a Warning: Moderate.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Heavy Rain Warning - Orange
entry.summary: Expect a further 150 to 200 mm of rain. Peak rates of 20 to 30 mm/h expected with thunderstorms possible. Impact: Streams and rivers may rise rapidly. Surface flooding, slips, and difficult driving conditions possible. Chance of upgrading to a Red Warning: Minimal.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Heavy Rain Warning - Orange
entry.summary: Expect a further 100 to 150 mm of rain about the main divide, and 60 to 100 mm within 20 km farther east. Peak rates of 20 to 30 mm/h expected about the main divide, with thunderstorms possible. Freezing level around 2200 metres. Impact: Streams and rivers may rise rapidly. Surface flooding, slips, and difficult driving conditions possible. Chance of upgrading to a Red Warning: Minimal.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Strong Wind Warning - Orange
entry.summary: Severe northwest gales, gusting 130 km/h in exposed places. Impact: Damage to trees, powerlines, and unsecured structures possible. Driving may be difficult, especially for high-sided vehicles and motorcycles. Chance of upgrading to a Red Warning: Minimal.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Road Snowfall Warning
entry.summary: The snow level is expected to lower during Sunday morning and afternoon to about 500 metres. Expect 2 to 8 cm of snow to accumulate.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Road Snowfall Warning
entry.summary: Rain could fall as snow at times about the summit from around midday Sunday. Less than 1 cm of snow may accumulate.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Road Snowfall Warning
entry.summary: Snow showers expected about and eastwards of the summit. 2 to 5 cm may accumulate above 400 metres.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Road Snowfall Warning
entry.summary: Snow showers expected about the summit from Sunday morning and 1 to 3 cm may accumulate there by dawn. Snow showers lowering to 400 metres early afternoon and a further 10 to 15 cm may accumulate about the summit with 1 to 3 cm down to 400 metres.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Road Snowfall Warning
entry.summary: Rain is expected to turn to snow near the summit tonight. From Saturday night to Sunday morning, 2 to 5 cm of snow is forecast to accumulate near the summit with lesser amounts down to 800 metres.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited
